---
title: "Sunoj Shajahan"
output:
  html_document:
    set_title: "My Title!"
    # toc: true
    theme: default
    # toc_float: true
---

```{r load_packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE} 
library(fontawesome)
```

![](./images/myphoto.jpg#right){width=45%}

<!-- # **Sunoj Shajahan** <br> -->

### Postdoctoral associate <br>
[Nutrient Management Spear Program](http://nmsp.cals.cornell.edu/), <br>
[Department of Animal Science](https://cals.cornell.edu/sunoj-shajahan), <br>
Cornell University, Ithaca, NY 14853. <br>
Email: ss2678[at]cornell.edu <br>

# [`r fa("github", fill = "black")`](https://github.com/sunojshajahan)     [`r fa("twitter", fill = "#1DA1F2")`](https://twitter.com/sunojshajahan)  [`r fa("researchgate", fill = "#0cb")`](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Sunoj-Shajahan-2)    

[Sunoj Shajahan CV](SunojCV_July5_2022.pdf)


### My research
I work on precision agriculture projects at NMSP, particularly on using remotely sensed imageries to estimate and predict corn grain and silage yield at the sub-field level. I make use of time-series aerial imageries from unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and low earth orbit satellites. Along with these imageries, I am interested in understanding the interaction between other data layers, such as weather, topography, and landforms, with the yield monitor data. My goal is to develop farm-specific yield models by training with multi-year yield monitor data and remotely sensed data layers. I am exploring big data analytics using cloud computing to analyze and build such models.

<!-- ![](./images/myphoto.jpg#right){width=45%} -->

### Updates

+ August 31, 2022: 
[Ben Yeh](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bhyeh/), Statistics and Computer Science graduate from Cornell University '2022, wrapped up his internship with NMSP. He worked on training a [LSTM neural network](https://www.esf.edu/stratus/stratus-2022-5-23.pdf#page=35) for yield prediction using aerial images captured from unmanned aerial system. 

+ August 22, 2022:
[Benjamin Polson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ben-polson-4730181b0/), majoring in Biometry & Statistics with minors in Computer Science and Horticulture, joined our team as an intern for the fall semester.   

+ August 19, 2022:
My intern, [Ibtida Ahmed](./images/Ibtida_1.jpeg), Junior, majoring in Computer Science at SUNY, Buffalo, completed his summer internship with us. He was productive during this stint and developed several Python pipelines for automated processing of geospatial data. Good luck for his new semester!.
<!-- ![](./images/Ibtida_1.jpeg#center){height=15%} -->

+ August 5, 2022:
The interns at NMSP organized a [picnic at Stewart Park](./images/Interns_social_Aug52022.jpg) on their last day with us for this summer semester.
<!-- ![](./images/Interns_social_Aug52022.jpeg#center){width=65%} -->

+ August 4, 2022:
Aileen Randolph from [New York Farm Viability Institute](https://nyfvi.org/) joined our [team meeting](./images/Aileen_NYFVI_Aug42022.jpeg) and gave us a talk about grant funding opportunities at NYFVI.

<!-- ![](./images/Aileen_NYFVI_Aug42022.jpeg#center){width=65%} -->

+ August 4, 2022:
Interns at NMSP wrapped up their stint with us with a [mini symposium](./images/Intern_wrap_Aug42022.jpeg).

+ May 20, 2022:
[Isha Vaish](https://www.linkedin.com/in/isha-vaish-2a0163208/), graduated with double major in Computer Science and Statistics. She worked with in our team since Spring 2021 and significantly contributed to our [NNYADP project](https://nnyagdev.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/NNYADPSatelliteDroneReportFINAL.pdf). She is currently pursuing a MS in Data Science at Harvard University.
<!-- ![](./images/Intern_wrap_Aug42022.jpeg#center){width=65%} -->

+ December 14, 2022:
[Yiran Li](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yiran-li-3a6a041ab/), graduated with Biometry and Statistics in Fall 2021. She worked with us with the NNYADP project along with Isha. She is currently pursuing masters degree in Bioinformatics at ETH Zurich. 


<!-- # My mentorship -->

<!-- ## Student 1 -->
<!-- ## Student 2 -->
<!-- ## Student 3 -->
<!-- ## Student 4 -->
